skill.name: journey-map
skill.description: Guides the creation of customer journey maps for user flows, workflows, pain points, emotions, and improvement opportunities.
skill.summary: Create structured customer journey maps for user experience analysis and workflow improvement.
skill.author: Dami-Randle

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as shell execution and ordinary words as weak cryptographic algorithms. Manual review found only documentation, example formatting, and skill usage guidance, with no executable code, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are fenced markdown examples or inline skill names, not Ruby or shell execution. No command invocation or user input execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is markdown documentation. The detected backticks delimit an emotion chart, output template, and inline references.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Terms
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain journey mapping prose and table headings. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic API, or security-sensitive algorithm use appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens occur in user experience documentation. There is no code path where cryptographic behavior could execute.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to see every step, feeling, and friction point in a user workflow. This skill creates structured journey maps that clarify user context, touchpoints, pain points, and improvements.
actual_capabilities: Defines user context, goals, triggers, and success criteria.
Breaks journeys into phases, steps, actions, and touchpoints.
Documents user thoughts, emotions, pain points, and opportunities.
Creates an emotion curve for the mapped experience.
Summarizes insights and prioritized improvement recommendations.
limitations: Requires user-provided journey details or research inputs.
Does not collect analytics, interview users, or validate assumptions by itself.
Uses generic journey phases unless the user supplies domain-specific stages.
Does not produce visual design files or interactive diagrams.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Flow
use_cases.description: Map the target journey before design begins, including user goals, emotions, risks, and improvement opportunities.

use_cases.title: Audit an Existing Workflow
use_cases.description: Document each workflow step to find friction, unclear touchpoints, and high-impact fixes.

use_cases.title: Align Service Teams
use_cases.description: Create a shared view of the customer experience across onboarding, support, compliance, or renewal workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journey Map
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with a simple user journey and limited context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a customer journey map for [persona] who wants to [goal]. Include phases, actions, emotions, pain points, and opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Workflow Pain Point Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze a known workflow for user friction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map this workflow from start to finish: [workflow details]. Identify pain points, emotional lows, and practical improvements for each step.

prompt_templates.title: Stakeholder Journey Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a concise journey map for review or planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a journey map for [journey name] and summarize the main insights, risks, and recommendations for a stakeholder audience.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Phase Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use richer inputs to create a detailed journey analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a detailed journey map using this research: [notes]. Include context, phases, touchpoints, emotion curve, key insights, and prioritized recommendations.

output_examples.input: Map the onboarding journey for a first-time compliance software user.
output_examples.output: A structured journey map with onboarding phases, user actions, emotions, pain points, and recommendations to reduce setup confusion.

output_examples.input: Analyze the workflow for uploading a supplier certificate.
output_examples.output: A workflow journey showing upload steps, system responses, user concerns, friction points, and opportunities to improve guidance.

output_examples.input: Document the renewal journey for expiring documents.
output_examples.output: A journey overview with trigger events, notification touchpoints, emotional changes, and prioritized improvements for renewal completion.

best_practices: Provide the persona, goal, trigger, and known workflow steps.
Include real user research, support tickets, or analytics when available.
Ask for prioritized recommendations when planning implementation work.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for direct user research.
Do not assume one journey represents every persona or segment.
Do not request final design decisions without validating the mapped assumptions.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured customer journey maps with phases, actions, emotions, pain points, insights, and recommendations.

faq.question: Can it analyze existing workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the workflow steps, and it can identify friction, missing touchpoints, and improvement opportunities.

faq.question: Does it need user research?
faq.answer: It works best with research inputs, but it can also create a draft from product or workflow descriptions.

faq.question: Can it produce visual diagrams?
faq.answer: It produces text-based journey maps and emotion curves. It does not create design files or interactive diagrams.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found documentation only, with no executable code, network activity, or prompt injection.
